zotero.abstractNote: Purpose: To introduce a novel method for magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) based on the concept of spread-spectrum (SS) communication. Methods: In SS-MRI, the radiofrequency (RF) excitation is modulated by a pseudo-random noise sequence, spreading the signal over a wide bandwidth. The image is reconstructed from the received signal using a matched filter or an iterative reconstruction algorithm. We demonstrate the feasibility of SS-MRI using numerical simulations and phantom experiments. Results: SS-MRI allows for high-speed imaging with reduced sensitivity to motion and susceptibility artifacts. The method is robust to noise and provides high-quality images even with low signal-to-noise ratios. Conclusion: SS-MRI is a promising technique for fast and robust MRI, with potential applications in clinical and research settings.
